<> In PHP, you can replace a URL in a string using the `str_replace()` function. Here's an example of how to do it: ```php $str = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $replaceUrl = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $str = str_replace("http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1", $replaceUrl, $str); echo $str; ``` This will output: ``` New post on the site http://yahoo.com/abc1

``` To make this work for repeating strings, you can use a loop or a regular expression with `preg_replace()` for more flexibility. Here's a simple example using `str_replace()`: ```php $str = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $replaceUrl = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $str = str_replace("http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1", $replaceUrl, $str); echo $str; ``` A palindrome in this context is a string that reads the same forwards and backwards. For example: ``` madam ``` This is a simple palindrome.
